text: ICU is indicated for all patients with acute spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage. ( 1 ) ( 8 ) ( 9 ) ( 10 ) If available, a neurocritical care unit with neurosurgical capabilities is preferred. ( 1 ) ( 11 ) ( 12 ) ( 13 ) See Intensive, Intermediate, and Telemetry Care Guidelines ISC . [ A in Context Link 1 ]
See Clinical Indications for Admission to Inpatient Care in this guideline. [ B in Context Link 1 ]
Cardiac arrhythmias may occur. ( 1 ) [ C in Context Link 1 ]
Intermittent compression devices should be given for DVT prophylaxis on admission. Pharmacologic prophylaxis may be considered after 24 to 48 hours of stability. ( 1 ) ( 14 ) [ D in Context Link 1 ]

A specialty society guideline recommends careful titration of antihypertensive therapy for patients with mild to moderate spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage whose SBP is between 150 and 220 mm Hg, with a goal of initiating treatment within 2 hours of onset and reaching a target range of 130 to 150 mm Hg. ( 1 ) The same guideline recommends against acutely lowering SBP to less than 130 mm Hg because of increased risks of acute renal injury and other adverse events. ( 1 ) This same guideline finds insufficient data to make recommendations for patients with SBP greater than 220 mm Hg or for large intracranial hemorrhage. ( 1 ) However, another guideline concludes, based on low-quality data, that the careful use of continuous IV medication under close blood pressure monitoring to lower blood pressure is reasonable for patients with SBP greater than 220 mm Hg. ( 15 ) Treatment of hypertension is also recommended if there is stroke with concomitant acute myocardial ischemia, aortic dissection, preeclampsia, or eclampsia. ( 16 ) [ E in Context Link 1 ]
Some patients may have their hydration needs met via alternative means (eg, percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy tube). [ F in Context Link 1 ]

For children with hemorrhagic stroke due to arteriovenous malformation, surgical resection or radiation therapy is preferred over embolization therapy due to high rates of hemorrhage post embolization. ( 4 ) Embolization is preferred as first-line therapy for most cases of arteriovenous fistula. ( 4 ) [ G in Context Link 1 ]
Discharge instructions should be given in the patient's and caregiver's native language using trained language interpreters whenever possible. ( 33 ) [ H in Context Link 1 ]
